questions.question: What is the purpose of selecting a dataset at the beginning of the experiment?

answers.a: To visualize the data distribution and inspect dataset properties before applying kernels
answers.b: To reduce the number of samples

explanations.a: Correct. Dataset selection allows users to view the scatter plot and dataset details before applying kernel methods.
explanations.b: Dataset selection does not reduce samples.
explanations.c: Model training occurs later during classification.
explanations.d: Class labels are necessary for classification tasks.

questions.question: Which classical kernels are available for comparison in the experiment?

explanations.a: Correct. The experiment allows the selection of Linear, Polynomial, and RBF kernels.
explanations.b: Logistic is not a kernel function.
explanations.c: These are machine learning algorithms, not kernels.

questions.question: What does the gamma parameter control when using the RBF kernel?

answers.a: The learning rate of the model
answers.b: The influence range of individual data points
answers.c: The number of features in the dataset
answers.d: The number of classes in the dataset

explanations.a: Gamma does not control learning rate.
explanations.b: Correct. Gamma determines how far the influence of a single data point reaches in the RBF kernel.
explanations.c: The number of features is determined by the dataset.
explanations.d: Gamma does not control class count.

questions.question: What is the main role of the quantum feature map in the experiment?

answers.b: To encode classical data into quantum states

explanations.a: Feature maps do not compress datasets.
explanations.b: Correct. Quantum feature maps encode classical data into quantum states using quantum circuits.
explanations.c: Feature maps do not remove features.
explanations.d: Labels are provided by the dataset.

questions.question: Which parameter determines how many quantum bits are used in the circuit?

explanations.a: Gamma is related to the RBF kernel.
explanations.b: Correct. The qubits slider controls the number of quantum bits used in the circuit.
explanations.c: Kernel type selects the classical kernel.
explanations.d: Dataset size does not control the quantum circuit.

questions.question: What does the Kernel Alignment Score indicate in the experiment?

answers.a: The training time of the model
answers.b: The similarity between classical and quantum kernel matrices
answers.d: The number of samples in the dataset

explanations.a: Kernel alignment does not measure training time.
explanations.b: Correct. It measures how similar the classical and quantum kernel structures are.
explanations.d: Dataset size is unrelated to the alignment score.

questions.question: Which machine learning model is used for classification in this experiment?

explanations.a: Correct. Both classical and quantum kernels are evaluated using an SVM classifier.
explanations.b: Decision Trees are not used in this experiment.
explanations.d: Naive Bayes is unrelated to kernel methods.

questions.question: What is the purpose of comparing classical and quantum classification accuracy?

answers.a: To determine which kernel representation performs better for the dataset
answers.b: To reduce the number of training samples
answers.d: To modify the quantum circuit automatically

explanations.a: Correct. The comparison shows how the quantum kernel performs relative to the classical kernel.
explanations.b: Accuracy comparison does not change dataset size.
explanations.d: The circuit configuration is set earlier in the experiment.
